title: Team & Access
lede: Bring your whole team into iMash — create a login for every person, hand each one exactly the pages, data, and prices they should see, and stay in full control with one-click enable, disable, and impersonation.

steps.title: Open Team & Access
steps.content:
Everything about your team lives on a single screen. Let's find it.

  1. In the left sidebar, click Team & Access (near the top, under Overview). You can also click the User Management tab in the header, or go directly to /user-management.
  2. The User Management page opens on the Users tab — a list of everyone on your account. The account owner shows an Owner badge; everyone you add is a sub-account (team member) under you.
  3. Note the five tabs across the top — you will use all of them in this guide:
    • Users — the people list: add members, assign roles, toggle features, transfer funds.
    • Roles — named permission sets you assign to members (what data they can read/write).
    • Permissions — the fine-grained editor for each role.
    • Groups — Permission Groups that decide whose records a member can see.
    • View Profiles — reusable presets of page visibility and pricing you can apply to many members at once.
💡 Only the account owner and members with the User Management permission can open this page — so you decide who gets to manage the team.

images.alt: User Management page open on the Users tab
images.caption: The Users tab lists the owner and every team member, with tabs for Roles, Permissions, Groups, and View Profiles

images.alt: User Management page open on the Users tab
images.caption: The Users tab lists the owner and every team member, with tabs for Roles, Permissions, Groups, and View Profiles

steps.title: Create a team member (sub-account)
steps.content:
Each team member gets their own email and password — never share one login.

  1. On the Users tab, click the blue Add User button.
  2. In the Create User window, fill in:
    • Email — the address the member will sign in with.
    • Password — their starting password (they can change it later).
    • First Name and Last Name.
    • Role (optional) — pick a role now or leave it blank and assign one later.
  3. Click Create User. The member appears in the list and can sign in immediately.
⚠️ New members start with sensible defaults, but always review their role and feature toggles (next steps) before handing over the login — especially Billing, Credentials, and User Management access.

images.alt: Create User modal with email, password, name, and role fields
images.caption: The Create User window — email, password, name, and an optional starting role

images.alt: Create User modal with email, password, name, and role fields
images.caption: The Create User window — email, password, name, and an optional starting role

steps.title: Understand the Users list
steps.content:
Every row is a member, and every column is a control. Here is what each one does:

  • User — name and sign-in email.
  • Role — the member's assigned role (Owner for the main account). "No role assigned" means they only get the default access.
  • Groups — the Permission Groups the member belongs to. Click the ✏️ pencil to add or remove groups right from the row.
  • View Profile — the View Profile applied to the member (or None). Click the ✏️ pencil to assign, change, or clear it.
  • Status — Active (can sign in) or Inactive (sign-in blocked).
  • Balance — the member's own calling balance. Click the + icon to transfer funds from your balance to theirs.
  • Created — when the account was created.
  • Billing (Margin) — the profit margin you add on top of platform prices for this member, and their billing mode: Independent (member spends their own balance) or Parent (usage is deducted from your main account). Click the ✏️ pencil to edit.
  • Est. Price — the estimated per-minute price the member effectively pays (hover for the regular / realtime / transcription breakdown).
  • Actions — per-member buttons for permissions, editing, impersonation, and enable/disable (covered in the last step).

images.alt: Users table showing role, groups, view profile, status, balance, billing margin, estimated price, and actions columns
images.caption: The full Users table — every column is a live control, from group membership to per-member pricing

images.alt: Users table showing role, groups, view profile, status, balance, billing margin, estimated price, and actions columns
images.caption: The full Users table — every column is a live control, from group membership to per-member pricing

steps.title: Create roles — start from a template
steps.content:
A Role is a named bundle of data permissions: what a member can read, create, update, or delete. Instead of building one from scratch, start from a ready-made template.

  1. Open the Roles tab.
  2. Browse the built-in templates — each has a Template badge and a description: Super Admin, Operations Manager, Agent (Contact Center), Monitor (Contact Center), Analyst, Developer, and Standard User.
  3. Click Use next to a template to turn it into a real, editable role on your account.
  4. Or click Create Role to build your own: optionally pick Base on Template (Optional), then enter a Role Name (e.g., "Sales Manager") and a Description, and click Create Role.
  5. The Users column shows how many members currently hold each role.
💡 Give everyone who does the same job the same role — change the role once and the whole team's access updates together.

images.alt: Roles tab listing role templates with Use buttons and user counts
images.caption: The Roles tab — ready-made templates like Agent, Analyst, and Operations Manager, one click away from becoming your own role

images.alt: Roles tab listing role templates with Use buttons and user counts
images.caption: The Roles tab — ready-made templates like Agent, Analyst, and Operations Manager, one click away from becoming your own role

steps.title: Fine-tune what a role can do with data
steps.content:
This is where you decide, area by area, exactly what a role can touch.

  1. Open the Permissions tab.
  2. On the left, click the role you want to edit. Each card shows how many users hold it and a quick "read / write" summary.
  3. On the right, the permission matrix appears, grouped by area — Communications, CRM, Payments, Campaigns & Dialing, AI & Tools, Calendar, WhatsApp, and User Management & API.
  4. For every item, tick or untick Read, Create, Update, and Delete. Use the All links in the group header to set a whole group at once.
  5. Set the Scope for each item:
    • Own — the member only sees and edits records they personally own.
    • All — the member sees records across the account (filtered by their Permission Groups, see the Groups step).
  6. Click Save Changes in the yellow bar that appears at the top.
⚠️ Changes are not applied until you click Save Changes — if you switch roles with unsaved edits, the warning banner reminds you first.

images.alt: Permissions tab with a role selected showing read, create, update, delete checkboxes and own or all scope per table
images.caption: The permission matrix — Read / Create / Update / Delete per area, with an Own or All scope switch on every row

images.alt: Permissions tab with a role selected showing read, create, update, delete checkboxes and own or all scope per table
images.caption: The permission matrix — Read / Create / Update / Delete per area, with an Own or All scope switch on every row

steps.title: Show or hide pages per member (feature toggles)
steps.content:
Roles control data; feature toggles control which pages and menus a member sees. Hide everything a person doesn't need and their sidebar stays beautifully simple.

  1. On the Users tab, click the Permissions button (the small grid icon, first in the Actions column) on a member's row.
  2. The Permissions window opens, mirroring your sidebar section by section:
    • General — Overview, Audit, System Health.
    • Intelligence Center — Smart Agents, Automation, Smart Studio, plus a master switch that hides the whole group.
    • Messages — SMS Messages, WhatsApp, Email, plus a master switch.
    • Call Center — Phone Numbers, PBX Extensions, SIP Providers, Call Logs, KnowledgeBase, Custom Tools, Do Not Call, Live Calls, Contact Center, Voicemail Settings — with a top-level master flag.
    • Campaign — AI Dialer, Human Dialer, Campaign Reports, Campaign Stats.
    • CRM — Dashboard, Accounts, Contacts, Leads, Settings, Calendar, Booking Forms, Project Kanban, Tasks, Deals, Invoices, Quotes, Services, Products, Subscriptions, Dispatch.
    • Account & Tools — API Keys, Credentials (shared LLM/TTS/STT keys), and User Management (access to this very page).
    • Display Options — Hide Billing Prices (member sees usage but no dollar amounts) and Hide Billing Page (hides the entire Billing menu).
  3. Flip any toggle On (visible) or Off (hidden), then click Save changes. The member's sidebar updates on their next page load.
💡 If the member is assigned a View Profile, this window shows a banner instead of toggles — the profile owns their settings. Unassign the profile to edit toggles individually, or edit the profile itself to change everyone on it at once.

images.alt: Per-user Permissions modal with feature toggles for every sidebar section
images.caption: The per-member Permissions window — every sidebar page gets its own on/off switch, with master switches per section

images.alt: Per-user Permissions modal with feature toggles for every sidebar section
images.caption: The per-member Permissions window — every sidebar page gets its own on/off switch, with master switches per section

steps.title: Split your team into Permission Groups
steps.content:
Permission Groups answer one question: whose records can this member see? A member whose role has All scope sees only records owned by people in their groups — perfect for splitting a big team into pods, branches, or client teams.

  1. Open the Groups tab and click New Group.
  2. Enter a Name (e.g., "East Coast Team") and an optional Description, then save.
  3. Select the group in the left list, then click Add Members and search members by name or email. You can also click Create User here to create a new member directly inside the group.
  4. Each member row shows its Type (Main or Sub); use the 🗑️ icon to remove someone — their data and role are unaffected.
  5. Notice the extra tabs on the group panel — Members Roles, Role Mapping, Inbound SIP Routing, Ring strategy, Wait & Hold experience, and Queue Settings. These turn a group into a live Contact Center team with its own inbound number, ring order, and hold queue — see the Contact Center guides for the full walkthrough.
💡 Members with no group see account-wide data (when their role scope allows it). Add the owner to a group if group members should also see records owned by the main account.

images.alt: Groups tab with a group selected showing its members and contact center setup tabs
images.caption: A Permission Group — members on the left panel decide data visibility, and the extra tabs make the group a Contact Center team

images.alt: Groups tab with a group selected showing its members and contact center setup tabs
images.caption: A Permission Group — members on the left panel decide data visibility, and the extra tabs make the group a Contact Center team

steps.title: Create a View Profile — one preset for many members
steps.content:
Setting toggles member by member works for a small team. View Profiles scale it: build the preset once, assign it to any number of members, and every future edit cascades to all of them instantly.

  1. Open the View Profiles tab and click New Profile.
  2. Give it a Name (e.g., "Support Agents") and a Description, then create it.
  3. The profiles list on the left shows each profile with how many members are assigned to it (e.g., "2 assigned"). Click a profile to open the editor.
⚠️ Assigning a profile overwrites the member's individual toggle settings — the profile becomes the single source of truth. Each member can be on at most one profile.

images.alt: View Profiles tab listing profiles with assigned member counts and a New Profile button
images.caption: The View Profiles tab — each profile shows how many members it controls

images.alt: View Profiles tab listing profiles with assigned member counts and a New Profile button
images.caption: The View Profiles tab — each profile shows how many members it controls

steps.title: Configure the profile: pages, home page, pricing, AI limits
steps.content:
The profile editor is a superset of the per-member Permissions window — plus a few powerful extras:

  1. Name / Description — rename the profile at any time.
  2. Default Home Page — where assigned members land after login (and when they click the logo). Hiding Overview? You must pick a different, enabled home page first.
  3. Feature Toggles — the same section-by-section page switches you saw per member (General, Intelligence Center, Messages, Call Center, Campaign, CRM, Payments, Account & Tools, Display Options). A toggle left "not overridden" keeps whatever each member already has; use Clear to return a toggle to that neutral state.
  4. Pricing & Billing Mode — override billing for everyone on the profile: Billing Mode (Don't override, Independent, or Parent), Price Per Minute (your profit margin on top of platform base), and STT-Only Price. Per-member rows in the Assigned Sub-Accounts list can still override the profile values individually.
  5. Agent Constraints — restrict which AI models members may use: Disallowed LLM Models, Disallowed TTS Providers, and Disallowed STT Providers.
  6. Assigned Sub-Accounts — add or remove the members this profile controls.
  7. Click Save. Every change cascades to all assigned members immediately.

You can also assign a profile without opening this tab — use the ✏️ pencil in the View Profile column of the Users list.

images.alt: View Profile editor showing default home page, feature toggles, pricing and billing mode, agent constraints, and assigned sub-accounts
images.caption: The profile editor — page visibility, default home page, pricing overrides, AI model limits, and the member list, all in one place

images.alt: View Profile editor showing default home page, feature toggles, pricing and billing mode, agent constraints, and assigned sub-accounts
images.caption: The profile editor — page visibility, default home page, pricing overrides, AI model limits, and the member list, all in one place

steps.title: Impersonate, edit, enable or disable a member
steps.content:
The Actions column on each member's row keeps day-to-day management one click away. From left to right:

  • Permissions (grid icon) — opens the per-member feature-toggle window from step 6.
  • Edit (pencil icon) — update the member's first name, last name, or role.
  • Impersonate (person-switch icon, owner only) — sign in as that member and see the app exactly as they do. Perfect for verifying access before they even log in, or for helping them hands-on. Use the header menu to return to your own account.
  • Permissions shortcut (lock icon) — jumps straight to the Permissions tab with the member's role pre-selected.
  • Disable (red ✕) / Enable (✓) — instantly block or restore the member's sign-in. Disabling never deletes anything: their records, history, and settings stay intact, ready for the moment you re-enable them.
💡 Someone leaving the company? Disable beats delete — access ends immediately, but every call log, deal, and job they owned remains in your account.

That's the full toolkit — your team is onboarded, scoped, and secured. 🎉

images.alt: Row action buttons: permissions, edit, impersonate, role permissions shortcut, and disable
images.caption: Per-member actions — Permissions, Edit, Impersonate, role shortcut, and Disable/Enable

images.alt: Row action buttons: permissions, edit, impersonate, role permissions shortcut, and disable
images.caption: Per-member actions — Permissions, Edit, Impersonate, role shortcut, and Disable/Enable

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaTitle: Team & Access - Users, Roles, Groups & View Profiles | iMash Documentation
metaDescription: Create team member sub-accounts, assign roles and data permissions, split visibility with Permission Groups, apply View Profiles with page toggles and pricing, and impersonate, enable, or disable users in iMash.
